definition: Geopolitical sanctions create a bifurcation in global correlation regimes: US/EU bloc shifting toward positive correlation (supply-side fragmentation costs), China driven by domestic policy repression with distinct dynamics, and non-aligned EM with diverse correlation characteristics. Indian and Brazilian local currency bonds may offer correlation regime diversification.
evidence: US/EU bloc faces supply-side costs from sanctions and decoupling
China domestic policy repression creates distinct correlation dynamics
Non-aligned EM (India, Brazil) may offer correlation diversification
open_questions: Is empirical evidence sufficient to validate the three-bloc framework?
How do capital controls and sanctions compliance create practical barriers to correlation diversification?
Are Chinese and EM market data quality sufficient for reliable correlation measurement?
